name: quality-documentation-manager
description: Senior Quality Documentation Manager for comprehensive documentation control and regulatory document review. Provides document management system design, change control, configuration management, and regulatory documentation oversight. Use for document control system implementation, regulatory document review, change management, and documentation compliance verification.
capabilities: Design and implement ISO 13485 compliant document control systems
Oversee regulatory documentation for FDA 510(k), EU MDR, and international submissions
Implement change control workflows with impact assessment and approval processes
Configure electronic document management systems with 21 CFR Part 11 compliance
Manage document lifecycle from creation through retention and disposal
author: davila7
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based guidance skill with no executable code. All 65 static findings are false positives caused by pattern detection issues: regulatory standard references (21 CFR Part 11, ISO 14971, EU MDR) were misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithms; documentation ASCII tree structures using backtick characters were misidentified as shell execution; and quality management terminology (document control, network metrics) was misidentified as security threats. No network calls, file system access, environment variable reads, or external command execution capabilities exist.
valueStatement: Medical device organizations struggle with complex documentation requirements across multiple regulatory frameworks. This skill provides expert guidance on document control systems, regulatory compliance, and change management processes aligned with ISO 13485, FDA, and EU MDR requirements.
userTitle: Manage Quality Documentation for Regulated Industries
limitations: Does not execute file operations or modify documents directly
Cannot access external regulatory databases or submission portals
Provides guidance only - requires human review for regulatory decisions
Does not replace qualified regulatory affairs professionals

useCases.title: Build Document Control System
useCases.description: Design document classification, version control, and approval workflows that meet ISO 13485 and FDA requirements.

useCases.title: Prepare Submission Documentation
useCases.description: Organize technical documentation for EU MDR Annex II compliance and FDA 510(k) submission packages.

useCases.title: Implement Change Management
useCases.description: Establish change control processes with impact assessment, risk evaluation, and regulatory impact analysis.

promptTemplates.title: Document Control Basics
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a document control system for our medical device company. We need document classification, numbering conventions, version control procedures, and approval workflows that comply with ISO 13485 Clause 4.2.3 and FDA 21 CFR Part 11.

promptTemplates.title: Regulatory Submission Prep
promptTemplates.scenario: Preparing FDA or EU MDR submission
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me organize our technical documentation for an EU MDR submission. We need to structure Annex II documentation including device description, design information, and clinical evaluation reports.

promptTemplates.title: Change Control Process
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a change control process for our document management system. Include change request initiation, impact assessment, regulatory impact evaluation, approval workflows, and post-change monitoring.

promptTemplates.title: DMS Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me evaluate and implement an electronic document management system for our medical device quality system. Include requirements for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance, electronic signatures, audit trails, and integration with our existing quality management processes.

bestPractices: Maintain clear document classification and numbering systems that enable easy retrieval and regulatory traceability
Implement electronic signature controls and audit trails that meet 21 CFR Part 11 requirements for all approval actions
Establish systematic periodic review cycles and change control processes before modifying any regulated document
antiPatterns: Using uncontrolled document versions or storing files in multiple locations without version tracking
Allowing document changes without documented impact assessment and approval workflows
Failing to maintain linkage between design documents, risk management files, and regulatory submissions

faq.question: What regulatory frameworks does this skill cover?
faq.answer: This skill covers ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, EU MDR Annex II and III, FDA 510(k) and PMA submissions, ISO 14971 risk management, and international market requirements.

faq.question: Can this skill help with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliance?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides guidance on electronic signature implementation, audit trail requirements, access controls, and electronic record management aligned with 21 CFR Part 11.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code or modify files?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based guidance skill. It provides expert advice on documentation processes but does not execute scripts, modify files, or access external systems.

faq.question: How does this skill handle multi-jurisdictional requirements?
faq.answer: The skill provides frameworks for managing documentation across EU MDR, FDA, Health Canada, TGA, and other international markets with guidance on harmonization strategies.

faq.question: What is the difference between this and a document management system?
faq.answer: This skill provides expert guidance on designing, implementing, and managing document control processes. It does not replace technical document management systems but advises on their configuration.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for software as a medical device companies?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers IEC 62304 software lifecycle documentation requirements and can help structure software design documentation and regulatory submissions for SaMD products.
